behavior_summary: U8P_PULCHNEW::look is a short 'U8P_PULCHNEW::look' (examine) handler for the object/NPC. When the player inspects this entity the script emits a single short bark: "bell ringer ".
Immediately after producing the bark the process yields/suspends; there is no branching, menus, or external interactions.
No global flags, parameters, or external calls are read or written by this script; it is self-contained and purely presentational.
notable_control_flow: Linear: entry -> emit Bark('bell ringer ') -> suspend/yield/terminate the U8P_PULCHNEW::look process.
No conditional branches, menus, or subsequent actions observed.
open_questions: The raw disassembly contained low-confidence 'Unknown' nodes (e.g., a lone '{' and 'suspend' token). Is there any additional teardown or engine interaction intended after the bark, or is the suspend purely to yield the U8P_PULCHNEW::look process?

Is this handler invoked only by the explicit player 'U8P_PULCHNEW::look' action, or also by an engine fast-area enter trigger in certain contexts? (Process type 0x0208 suggests a U8P_PULCHNEW::look/inspect handler.)
The bark text includes a trailing space ("bell ringer "). Is the trailing space meaningful (concatenation target) or an artifact of extraction?

main_function.name: U8P_PULCHNEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspection/description response when the player looks at this NPC/object; display the short bark text and then suspend the process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game's U8P_PULCHNEW::look/inspect action for this NPC/object (process type 0x0208).
May also run in contexts where the engine invokes U8P_PULCHNEW::look-type processes (e.g., fast-area/inspection entry) but canonical data marks it as a U8P_PULCHNEW::look handler.

function_map.name: U8P_PULCHNEW::look
function_map.role: Handles the entity's response to being examined; emits a single bark and suspends.
